data.body:
CONTAINER REVIEW — G4 (child consistency) / parent 5fd2-a7c2-0aec-48fa × child 6c5c (Docs). Whole-roster cross-check applied.

VERDICT: one high-confidence G4 finding (conflicting-assumptions + residual-reference modes). The other 6 interaction modes are clean for this docs-only child.

FINDING — child's "kill-switch" framing contradicts the parent's settled design.
- criteria: [G4]
- location: child 6c5c §What ("the global enable/disable kill-switch") + §Acceptance Criteria AC#1 ("config (incl. the disable kill-switch)") vs parent 5fd2 Success Criterion #4 + §"Design decisions revisited" #1.
- evidence: (1) Parent SC#4 explicitly settles the opposite: "'rollback' is simply turning the preference off — an ordinary config preference, NOT a deploy-time kill-switch, and no separate rollback mechanism is needed." (2) Sibling ce75 (kill-switch/disable-config) was FORCE_CLOSED won't-do/superseded on this exact settlement: "config-gating IS the kill-switch/rollback ... No separate rollback mechanism is needed" — so the roster has already adjudicated "no separate kill-switch"; the child's surviving "kill-switch" wording is a residual reference to a mechanism the epic removed by design. (3) On-ticket corroboration: prior G6 (×3) and T8 Pass-1 reviews independently flagged the same docs-vs-behavior terminology divergence. The disable is the opt-in preference verify.require_plan_review_for_claim (DEFAULT OFF), owned/enforced by sibling 092b.
- scenarios: An author working strictly from the ticket text (not the shipped doc) writes a "config surface incl. the global enable/disable kill-switch" section; a client reads the config/CI-rigor docs and over-trusts a non-existent deploy-time kill-switch / separate rollback path.
- impact: CONSISTENCY defect in the plan text (NOT a coverage gap — the disable-config topic IS covered). The ticket §What + AC#1 are misaligned with parent SC#4 and with the won't-do disposition of sibling ce75. NOTE the live divergence is in the TICKET TEXT only: the SHIPPED doc (docs/plan-review-gate.md:176-197) already reconciles this correctly — documents the disable AS verify.require_plan_review_for_claim, default off, "Turning it off is the rollback — an ordinary preference, no kill-switch needed."
- checklist_item: "[ ] Reword child 6c5c §What + AC#1 to drop the 'kill-switch' framing and describe the disable as the opt-in config PREFERENCE (verify.require_plan_review_for_claim, DEFAULT OFF; turning it off IS the rollback), matching parent SC#4 and the won't-do disposition of sibling ce75 — so the ticket text agrees with the shipped doc."
- suggested_fix: Replace "the global enable/disable kill-switch" / "the disable kill-switch" in §What and AC#1 with "the enable/disable config preference (verify.require_plan_review_for_claim, defaults OFF; disable = turn the preference off, which IS the rollback — no separate kill-switch)." The shipped doc already does this; align the ticket text to it.

CLEAN ON THE OTHER MODES:
- Implicit shared state: none — docs-only artifact, no shared runtime/data state with siblings.
- Dependency gap: the workflow-engine usage doc is owned by EXTERNAL epic 6f2d (not a sibling in this roster); the child handles the not-yet-existing docs/workflow-engine.md by cross-referencing the current authoritative refs (workflow-authoring-v2.md / workflow-editor.md) and naming 6f2d as the pending consolidator — a deferral, not a sibling-ordering gap.
- Scope overlap: none — docs-only; the canonical workflow-engine intended-use doc is explicitly carved out to 6f2d; the CI-rigor concern is documented here as the *pattern* while the *mechanism* stays with 4bb7/092b.
- Ordering violation: a docs child landing after the implementation siblings is the expected order; no encoded-edge violation.
- Consumer impact: none — no downstream consumer of a docs artifact beyond readers.

SECONDARY NOTE (sub-threshold; not a separate G4 finding): the child juxtaposes "How the gate works (three-pass)" with a separate "Pass-4 affirmative COACH" deliverable without directing the author to reconcile the three-vs-four tension for the reader. This is NOT a parent/sibling contradiction — the parent itself frames it as "three passes + a coach" (Pass-4 is AFTER the gate, advisory, never alters the Pass-3 decision), and the shipped doc renders it that way. Recorded for completeness only (already flagged by the on-ticket T8 review).

G4 NET: one real parent↔child consistency finding (the "kill-switch" wording, contradicting parent SC#4 and the won't-do sibling ce75; live in the ticket text though already reconciled in the shipped doc). All other interaction modes clean.
